lede: The AirTrain connects passengers to terminals, airport parking lots, the hotel shuttle area, the rental car center, and public transportation.Learn about the JFK AirTrain

paragraphs: Each terminal offers different services and amenities, lounges, shops, food and more. Find details about your terminal and which airlines operate out of each, as well as security times updated in real time.

Terminal 1 serves several major international airlines, including Air France, Turkish Airlines, Lufthansa, Swiss and Korean Air. Travelers will find essential amenities, convenient dining, and duty-free shopping.
Many airlines operate out of Terminal 4, including Delta, Avianca, Virgin Atlantic, Emirates, LATAM and more. Check out current wait times, as well as lounges available, shops and restaurants.
Airlines that operate out of Terminal 5 are JetBlue, Cape Air, and Sun Country Airlines. Check out current wait times, as well as shops and restaurants available.
Many airlines operate out of Terminal 7 including Frontier, Aer Lingus, All Nippon, Air Canada, Norse, and more. Check out current wait times, as well as shops and restaurants available.

Many airlines operate out of Terminal 8, including American Airlines, British Airways, Alaska Airlines, Qatar, Iberia, and more. Check out current wait times, as well as shops and restaurants available.

blocks.h2: Everything you need for your travels through JFK airport
h3s: Grab a snack, pick up a gift, or take a break
Find carts, cash, and baggage help
Airport hotels for a quick stay
paragraphs: If you’re changing planes at JFK or traveling to another airport nearby, the Connections Guide has helpful tips to make your trip easier.
Need a ride to or from JFK? Learn about taxis, shuttles, rental cars, public transit, and parking options to help you get where you’re going.
Before you leave JFK, stop by for a quick bite, grab a last-minute gift, or relax at one of our lounges or shops.
After you arrive at JFK, you can find luggage carts, ATMs, porter services, and help with baggage issues. For lost or delayed bags, contact your airline first, then visit a baggage office if needed.
Here for a quick trip? Make it easier by staying nearby and book a stay at an airport hotel.
The Port Authority of New York and New Jersey 4 World Trade Center, 150 Greenwich Street New York, NY 10007 +1 (212) 435-7000
